newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. The 2026 EBIT-margin guidance cut to 8-10% (weaker high-margin Automated Products, higher cost base, H1 EBIT ~60% of prior year) was already known and explicitly referenced in the June 9 prior case. Order/revenue growth guide of 15-20% intact; +1.1% move is noise.

sources.label: Kardex 2026 guidance and business update (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent under-covered AI supply chain: focused warehouse-automation/shuttle/light-goods asset, AI-levered via orchestration software. Margin-cut already absorbed before Jun 9; no new info in window. Top-line guide 15-20% supports the automation-cycle thesis. Hold.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Intralogistics automation cycle; bookings momentum (FY25 +24%); light-goods robotics expansion
verdicts.primaryRisk: 2026 EBIT margin cut to 8-10% on growth investments; H1 EBIT ~60% of prior year
verdicts.bull: Reshoring/labor-scarcity drives 15-20% order/revenue growth guide; robotics-levered automation pure-play.
verdicts.neutral: Strong top-line but margin reset; AI link is via orchestration software, not platform exposure.
verdicts.bear: Margin guidance cut, -18% YTD, heavy opex investment compresses near-term profit; not an AI platform.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict screen: ASRS supporting secure-parts-logistics/robotics-enabled facilities is a capability claim untouched by the margin cut (a P&L event), and the page already took a ~20-place demotion. Re-penalizing for known earnings news would double-count near the screen bottom. Hold.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ASRS as backbone for secure parts logistics / robotics-enabled facilities
verdicts.primaryRisk: Speculative AI linkage; margin pressure; -18% YTD; not on US screen tier
verdicts.bull: Automated storage/retrieval is foundational to robotics-enabled facilities; durable intralogistics demand.
verdicts.neutral: Capability claim intact but speculative; bottom-of-screen rank already reflects the discount.
verdicts.bear: AI-node pathway is a thesis not revenue; margin cut and weak Automated Products segment weigh.
